programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: All you need in the kitchen for your precision slicing and dicing of fruits and vegetables. Including three cutting inserts, a finger protector, blade holder and a free docking station.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A medical student is murdered - and Dr Sloan suspects it is only a matter of time before the killer strikes again. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The medic investigates the suspicious deaths of several homeless people, who are seemingly being killed for their organs at a clinic funded by a friend. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: A blind girl with a vicious disposition almost costs Kimble his freedom. Guest starring Tuesday Weld

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry resorts to some clever - and perfectly legal - skulduggery when a woman who has escaped from a sanatorium is accused of murdering her ex-husband.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part one of two. Walker helps a man come to terms with his past in order to carry on his father's legacy. Wilma Knight makes a cameo appearance alongside son Chuck Norris

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The death of a seemingly happy and healthy bride moments before her wedding leaves Mac and Danny with a complex puzzle, especially as the doves due to be released after the ceremony have also been killed. Meanwhile, Stella and Aiden track down the corpse of a man who apparently chewed off his own hand, and struggle to ascertain the significance of a hole next to the body. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Kidnappers strike on Halloween, wounding a marine sergeant and snatching his daughter - leaving the team members wondering why anyone would try to extort money from someone on such a modest salary. As the agents begin searching for a motive, Abby surprises everyone by turning up at the lab in a revealing fancy-dress costume

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen's cover is blown while he is working undercover and he sends a coded text to Hetty, who effectively cuts him off from NCIS as she makes it the team's top priority to discover who found out his real identity and how. A man called Eugene Keelson contacts Callen and threatens to reveal information about his past unless he runs an errand for him, which involves delivering a flash drive to Bulgarian gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: The unconscious patient in a hospital in Decatur, Illinois, is really Richard Kimble, a victim of amnesia who does not remember he is innocent

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman's former boyfriend is suspected of killing both her and the much older man she had begun seeing - but as Stone and Robinette begin to prepare a prosecution case, Logan and Cerreta become increasingly convinced that he is innocent

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An enigmatic killer shoots dead a businessman spending Thanksgiving at a hotel in the city, then takes his glasses, jewellery and ID to Washington Dulles airport, which has been brought to a stand-still by a massive snowstorm. As the murderer's actions take effect, a terror alert is issued, leaving all northern US airports in a state of panic, and leaving it up to DiNozzo, Bishop and her husband - all of whom are trapped at Dulles by the weather - to bring the armed villain to justice. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part two of two. Walker helps a man carry on his father's legacy by confronting his past

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An estate agent and two clients discover the body of Lt Com Maya Burris, a naval attache who had been working in Egypt, and her detective husband Nick arrives on the scene to demand answers. Using documents discovered on the victim's boat, Abby links her to Barry Norton, who is suspected of having sold government secrets to a Middle Eastern terrorist group known as the SRC

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Keelson is found to have had a huge file labelled 'Callen, G', but it is destroyed remotely before Callen can retrieve it. The team is assigned to monitor Iranian terrorists trying to get their hands on a huge hidden fortune that belonged to the former shah, and the case appears to have a concrete link to Callen when a woman in the group's ranks is identified as being his sister Amy. Starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs goes into his favourite diner for a cup of coffee and finds himself staring down the barrel of a gun, forcing him to question choices he has made in the past and present. The action then flashes back a few days to when he and the rest of the team members were investigating the death of a petty officer on board USS Gentry, which has been undergoing repairs in dry dock. The 200th episode of the crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A special agent disappears during an undercover assignment, forcing the team to work alongside the LAPD in a case that involves human-trafficking, money-laundering and drugs. Starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The bodies of five people, all of whom are identified as being from the US Navy, are found on the floor of a diner, and all are connected to a suspicious tax firm that specialises in helping people who are in arrears. When further killings are linked to the business, the team realises the crime seem to be copycats of previous cases. Meanwhile, two of Gibbs' ex-wives appear at the location with something important to discuss with the agent. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team searches for a top-secret CIA aircraft when two agents end up in Area 51 during a routine mission, while Walter plans to upload Megan's consciousness onto a hard drive

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when three dehydrated survivors from a helicopter crash are discovered. The pilot is initially missing, but his body later washes ashore with a gunshot wound to the head. Things get complicated when one of the crew members tells the agents the chopper was hit by another aircraft, while another states that the pilot tried to kill them all by crashing into the sea on purpose. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. The owner of the vehicle turns out to be Vijay Chaya, the billionaire head of a large corporation, and the team suspects a case of mistaken identity. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. First lieutenant Michael Torres gets involved in a fight before falling to the ground, and is found beaten to death the next morning. The only witness is his friend and colleague Captain Joe Westcott, who suffers from post-traumatic stress disorder and has trouble distinguishing reality from his past military experiences in the Middle East

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tony reviews satellite footage of rebel forces in the Middle East and spots Kersey among a group of insurgents, concluding that Joe Westcott's fear of him was well-founded. Back at HQ, Abby creates a 3D computer model of the attack that occurred in Afghanistan and Joe dons a virtual-reality headset to relive the traumatic events. Will it jog his memory?

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens

The victim is revealed to be Navy lieutenant Dean Massey, who DiNozzo Sr had threatened to kill after being cut out of a land development deal. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy captain Jake Marsden is murdered in his hotel room and inquiries lead to marine lieutenant Emma Reynolds - who tells the team she was the intended target. After being stationed in Kabul, she became pregnant by a local man called Amir who was later killed in action - and Abby discovers he was the sole male heir of a wealthy family that owns a large tract of land in a politically sensitive part of Afghanistan

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy commander last seen in the company of a mysterious blonde woman, who turns out to be missing NCIS agent and Tony's ex-lover EJ Barrett. She identifies the murderer as Seal-turned-assassin Marcellus Dane, who is suspected of working for Casey Stratton - a member of black ops unit Phantom Eight. Guest starring Sarah Jane Morris

programme.desc: Anna Kirkwall starts late on the first day of her new job at Skelthwaite Health Centre. Meanwhile, preparations for Jake's christening are under way, and Simon is in Australia trying to persuade Ruth to come home with their son Alfie

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe investigates a psychic's claims to have received a sack of poison-pen letters, and concludes her apparent plight is all a cheap publicity stunt - until he uncovers a possible link between the hate mail and a local girl's death. Offbeat crime drama, starring Richard Griffiths, Maggie Steed and Selina Cadell

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe has cause to regret getting involved in an elderly cookery writer's family affairs, and a series of squabbles among the staff only add to his woes. Comedy drama, starring Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Richard Sharpe and the faithful Harper begin their latest tour of duty by uncovering top-level corruption in the Army. Reporting their findings leads to attempts on their lives, so they fake their own deaths and re-enlist under new identities in an attempt to expose the culprits. Guest starring Julian Fellowes and featuring Alexander Armstrong in an early role

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The sinister Family searches for the Doctor, while he struggles to come to terms with his destiny as a Time Lord. Meanwhile, both Martha and Joan are forced to take desperate measures to save the whole of history. Sci-fi adventure, starring David Tennant, Freema Agyeman and Jessica Hynes

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: A woman receives a series of cryptic messages from the past left for her by the Doctor, as she tries to solve the mystery of the Weeping Angels, a terrifying group of statue-like creatures lurking in an abandoned house. Guest starring Carey Mulligan alongside David Tennant and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of three. Captain Jack Harkness throws himself back into the Doctor's life as the Time Lord tries to stop the Tardis hurtling out of control. Eventually the ship lands on a distant planet at the end of the universe, where a lonely professor is trying to save his people from extinction. Derek Jacobi (Gladiator) guest stars, with John Barrowman reprising his role from the first series and spin-off show Torchwood

programme.title: The 39 Steps
programme.desc: Rupert Penry-Jones stars in this adaptation of John Buchan's classic novel, set in 1914. Engineer Richard Hannay is bored and restless in London - until he meets Scudder, who claims to be a British spy. When the agent is killed in his flat, Hannay is thrown headlong into adventure, forced to go on the run under suspicion of murder. Fleeing to Scotland, with police and spies on his tail, he teams up with feisty suffragette Victoria Sinclair, and although they fail to hit it off, the couple become increasingly reliant on each other as they race to unmask a traitor and save Great Britain from German invasion. Action thriller, co-starring Eddie Marsan and Lydia Leonard

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Kristin, Breen and Gina witness a fatal skydiving accident while celebrating Mike's birthday with a picnic. To make matters worse, Kristin knows the victim - entrepreneur Andre Barrington, an ex-boyfriend. When the cords are found severed, it becomes clear that his demise was either suicide or murder

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The offer of a substantial reward inspires Hetty to find the bright spark behind a spate of arson attacks, and she begins by investigating the activities of a neighbour's foster daughter whose absences from home coincide with the fires. Housewife-detective series, starring Patricia Routledge, with Derek Benfield and Dominic Monaghan

programme.title: Rillington Place
programme.desc: After the strain of Timothy Evans' trial, Christie finds it increasingly difficult to maintain the charade. As his marriage breaks down he embarks upon a course of action that finally reveals his true nature to the world. Fact-based drama, starring Tim Roth, Samantha Morton, Nico Mirallegro and Jodie Comer

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki travels to Mexico to attend the funeral of Luisa, a former intern at the Lyell Centre who was murdered in a carjacking in her native country. She soon stumbles across the post-mortem report and realises that the body is not that of her ex-colleague. Assisted by Dr Eva Vasquez and fired up with the idea that Luisa might still be alive, Nikki and Jack trace her last-known movements and are led to a farm where a cartel massacre was carried out. Crime drama, starring Emilia Fox

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The sinister Family searches for the Doctor, while he struggles to come to terms with his destiny as a Time Lord. Meanwhile, both Martha and Joan are forced to take desperate measures to save the whole of history. Sci-fi adventure, starring David Tennant, Freema Agyeman and Jessica Hynes

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A heartrending death devastates some of the Towers residents. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Lifestyle presenters Piers Taylor, Tom Dyckhoff, Esme Young, Danny Clarke and Keith Brymer Jones take on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the south Wales countryside to help a couple with £500,000 to spend on relocating from Newport to a village community. While in the region, Nicki visits the only whisky distillery in Wales that uses the mineral-rich water of the Brecon Beacons - to potent effect

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich meet Jason and Emily, who want a bohemian fairy garden to match their 18th-century Bristol home

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton travel across Derbyshire and Leicestershire, where items of interest include 18th-century carvings and a bowl full of marbles. Along the way Catherine gets steamed up on the heritage Great Central Railway and James visits a pottery

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Jessie Wallace and Lacey Turner, better known to fans of the show as Kat and Stacey, for a Slater family reunion. They talk about their characters' most memorable moments, reveal some of their best behind-the-scenes stories and give an idea of what's to come when the soap returns next week

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back at when the celebrities have been pushed to the limits, from trying to impress some of the best chefs in the country to the most daunting challenge of all, the Chefs' Table. They also recall a couple of memorable challenges, including a final week task from 2012 in which Christine Hamilton, Dick Strawbridge and Lisa Faulkner had to prepare a fine dining lunch from a tiny kitchen on the Orient Express

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man is found murdered on his boat five miles out at sea, with no possible way a killer could have escaped. At first Humphrey turns his attention to the small group he was with the night before he died - but as the investigation deepens, the investigation takes an unexpected turn that requires the team to leave the comfort of their Caribbean island - and go searching for their suspects in London. Kris Marshall stars

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a selection of eight of the most extraordinary sequences from Planet Earth II and Blue Planet II, with a new soundtrack by Hans Zimmer, Jacob Shea and the team at Bleeding Fingers - performed by the BBC Concert Orchestra and Mercury Award-winning rapper Dave on the grand piano. The film ranges from the East Cape of South Africa to the north of Norway and from Chile to the Indian Ocean to show how animals overcome adversity to survive and thrive in some of the world's most challenging environments

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason and Adam Fleming present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Most people who go missing are reported to police within a few hours, but for those who live on the streets it isn't always so straightforward. Aaron hasn't been seen for two months when the homeless healthcare centre that usually treats him notifies Gloucestershire Constabulary that he is a missing person. Officers scour the streets and homeless shelters but can find no trace of him. The town's rough sleepers know him well, but no one has any idea what may have happened to him. It's not long before another drug user is added to the missing priority list - James, who is of no fixed address, has discharged himself from hospital against medical advice. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Jon Kay presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: It is the tiger cubs' first birthday bash, and Ben Fogle and Kate Humble look for signs that Longleat Safari Park's Southern Koala breeding programme - the only one in Europe - has resulted in a joey. Meanwhile, Jean Johansson joins keeper James to take a closer look at the anatomy of Gwen - a huge hairy tarantula

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is in Croydon, south London, at a basement flat bought by two brothers. Martel Maxwell visits a colourful terrace in Cumbria, while Dion Dublin is in Greater Manchester, where a first-time developer has taken on a real challenge

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the UK's most remote hospital. There are serious concerns for a baby boy who is brought into A&E feeling very unwell

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles arrives in Peterborough for one of the largest antiques fairs in the UK. He's joined by Bargain Hunt regular Colin Young, who has some top tips for porcelain collectors. Experts Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell guide the red and blue teams around the fair, with each side hoping to buy low and sell high

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: With the community grieving, some residents consider leaving, and Watto has the last word at the wake. Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke star

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Soap actors Lisa Maxwell, Ayden Callaghan, Sean Wilson, Scott Wright and Steven Arnold compete in a charity edition hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple who have £500,000 to make a much-yearned-for leap to a country life on the Welsh borders. Along the way, the presenter visits 17th-century Tredegar House, now home to local artists' studios, and lends a hand making traditional Welsh musical instruments

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich try to breathe life into a concrete courtyard in Greater Manchester. Nick and Dinah want cottage plants, vegetable beds and room for family meals

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: James Braxton and Catherine Southon hit the final leg of their challenge, travelling through Cambridgeshire and Suffolk before heading to auction at Lincolnshire. Which one of them will make the greater profit?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich assist Emma and Steve, whose front garden in Nottinghamshire overlooking the River Trent, feels very much 'on display' to passers-by. The couple want greater privacy without losing the precious river view and also wish to utilise a half-boat that they own in the design. Both Charlie and the siblings propose a similar clever idea to solve the privacy problem, a sunken courtyard garden. This only makes it harder for Emma and Steve to decide which design to pick

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley presents a compilation of the best unseen moments from her time interviewing Walford's biggest stars

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker asks the questions in the light-hearted quiz, with celebrity guests AP McCoy, Ugo Monye, Jade Jones and Gary Pallister joining team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: Bolton's funniest stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is disappointed to discover she is not invited to Maria's hen party because of her age, and decides to gatecrash the event, only to end up at the wrong venue. She then tries to impress the bride-to-be's mother during a dinner date, but is so nervous she turns to drink, and the evening descends into chaos. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy (2011)
programme.desc: George Smiley, the deputy to the head of British intelligence, is forced into retirement after an operation ends in disaster with the death of an MI6 agent. Smiley is later recruited by the government to return to duty and investigate the possibility of a Soviet mole in a high-ranking position within MI6. Cold War thriller based on John le Carré's 1974 novel, starring an Oscar-nominated Gary Oldman, alongside Colin Firth, Tom Hardy, John Hurt, Benedict Cumberbatch, Toby Jones, Mark Strong, Ciaran Hinds and Roger Lloyd Pack

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by podcast-presenting couple Chris and Rosie Ramsey and chef Yotam Ottolenghi. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson treats her supper guests to a feast of lamb chops with mint, chilli and potatoes, served alongside roasted red onions with fennel and basil. Dessert is a true Anglo-Italian affair with a plum and amaretti crumble. There are also recipes for farro risotto with mushrooms, and chocolate pasta with a dark butterscotch sauce

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is by the seaside in Norfolk, Martel Maxwell encounters a house with no stairs in Lancashire and Dion Dublin is in Staffordshire at a home with a carpeted bath

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of day two of the annual event, which this year takes place at Manchester Regional Arena. Holly Bradshaw is aiming to win her eighth national pole vault title and local favourite Naomi Ogbeta will hope to make an impact in the triple jump, while the men's and women's 200m finals take place on the track. With commentary from Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Alice Through the Looking Glass (2016)
programme.desc: Alice Kingsleigh has spent three years sailing the high seas. Upon her return to London from China, she returns to the magical world of Underland and takes a journey back in time to save the Mad Hatter and her friends. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Johnny Depp and Anne Hathaway

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a psychiatrist from London, a drama student from Leicester, and a bus driver from Derbyshire

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a couples special of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants including Harry Judd, Izzy Judd, Ade Adepitan, Elle Exxe, Dan Renton Skinner, Olivia Lee, Nik Speakman and Eva Speakman

programme.title: Invictus (2009)
programme.desc: Following his election as president of South Africa, Nelson Mandela finds himself the leader of a racially and economically divided nation, and seeks a means to unite the people. The country's entry in the 1995 Rugby World Cup provides a perfect opportunity, and he enlists the aid of captain Francois Pienaar in making his team an inspiration to all South Africans. Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Morgan Freeman and Matt Damon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Nikki Fox, Miles Jupp, Nish Kumar and Joe Lycett to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Z for Zachariah (2015)
programme.desc: A woman lives alone on a farm that belonged to her family in the aftermath of a nuclear apocalypse, believing herself to be the last survivor of the human race. Her simple life is disrupted by the appearance of a second survivor, and they adapt to working together - but the arrival of a third individual threatens everything they have built. Sci-fi drama based on Robert C O'Brien's novel, starring Margot Robbie, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Chris Pine

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble meets a zebra in crisis, Ben Fogle plays Sherlock Holmes to find an escaped porcupine, and Jean Johansson learns what happens when a lion comes face to face with itself

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create a colourful, exotic sculpture garden in a home in Llandrindod Wells for Lynn and Bill

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: This time, Michael Portillo ventures on a rail journey through Germany, guided by Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide published in 1936. Starting in the vibrant city of Berlin, Michael visits the Museum of Modern Art in Berlin's Kreuzberg, where he sees how a leading artist of the era, George Grosz, warned of the rise of fascism in a haunting self-portrait. In the Schöneberg district of the capital, Michael researches the decadent night scene of the 1920s, and looks at how cabaret culture is being revived today. When he reaches Weimar, Michael investigates the beginnings of Bauhaus design and visits the movement's first building, before travelling onto Stuttgart, where he takes a ride in a high-performance Porsche to the manufacturer's Stuttgart headquarters

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Newlyweds the Castlings sample life in Perth for a week, but while Cheryl is keen to make a fresh start her husband Adam has begun to have doubts die to his career taking off

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Lizzie Daly and Rachel Parris for another test of wildlife knowledge, hosted by Kate Humble

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Robshaws are transported to the 1970s and Mary Berry is on hand to help the family stock up their brand new chest freezer, as frozen and convenience food became a life saver for time-pressed working women of the decade. Giles Coren meets the two hippies whose adventures in health food boosted the popularity of houmous, while the family has a go at self-sufficiency and discovers that milking a goat is nowhere near as simple as it looks

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Robshaws and their home are given a 1980s makeover, with a gadget-filled kitchen including an enormous microwave oven. Giles Coren and food historian Polly Russell use the national food survey to guide the family's diet and introduce them to the culinary treats and trends of the decade, with technology giving them the chance to make their own fizzy drinks and produce a cheese and ham toastie. They also learn about the rise of both nouvelle cuisine and the fast-food chains, and find a host of new tastes and flavours from around the globe now available at their local supermarket

programme.title: Animals Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Mongoose start wars to create a diversion for sneaky mating, while macaques kidnap babies to curry favour with their rivals. These behaviours prompt Liz Bonnin to investigate the animals who will stop at nothing to find a mate and raise a family. Liz reveals the fascinating science that lies behind some extraordinary tactics. In Tanzania, she finds out that sibling rivalry can actually benefit a whole hyena clan, and on the North American plains learns how promiscuity can be the key to having healthy prairie-dog pups. However, the presenter also discovers that such tactics are often the key to survival

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The experts assess items brought to the Blickling estate in Norfolk, and Paul Martin learns about Lord Lothian's key role in getting America involved in the Second World War

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The Rev Daniel Reed from Cornwall shows his prize-winning ducks and Gabbie Franklin from Herefordshire exhibits her chickens at the Pembrokeshire County Show in Wales

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Veteran broadcasters John Sergeant and Peter White take a road trip through Surrey on a search for antiques, with expert help from James Braxton and Philip Serrell. Along the way, John takes to the river in a historic yacht and Peter - blind from birth - bats a good innings at one of the country's oldest cricket clubs

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The three finalists take part in their biggest professional assignment to date, delivering their own masterclass at one of the world's most famous department stores, Harrods. Taking it in turns, they must each showcase a signature look concentrating on the eyes, explaining their techniques and delivering great make-up in front of an audience of industry professionals. They next day the artists face their final creative brief, on the theme of evolution, before the judges decide which two will compete in the final face off

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A selection of highlights from the stand-up show, featuring a different topic each week. Here, comedians Jason Byrne, Marlon Davis, Micky Flanagan, Jessica Fostekew, Guz Khan, Sean Lock and Sindhu Vee perform routines about children

programme.title: Semi-Detached
programme.desc: A journalist accompanies Kate home to write a lifestyle piece on her as the local MP. What they find is Ted and Stuart half-dressed, Madonna dressed as a tomato and everyone acting very suspiciously. Stuart tries his best to keep things together for his ex-wife, only for his own problems to arise with the return of April's former boyfriend, casting doubt over Bertha's paternity. Lee Mack stars in the real-time comedy drama, with Samantha Spiro, Patrick Baladi, Clive Russell and Christian Brassington

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian tries to help make sense of bewildering times as he dissects the week's news using stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction. Throughout the series he is joined by Sara Pascoe, Miles Jupp, Sophie Duker and a variety of other guests, beginning with Guz Khan and Sara Barron

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: An anxious young woman attempts to cope with the two personifications of her inner thoughts - one which encourages her to be impulsive and another that fears everything. Written by and starring Gbemisola Ikumelo

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer are joined by Feargal Sharkey for a trip to the River Lea in Hertfordshire, where Paul used to fish for perch with his friends when he was a teenager. Bob reads up on the wise words of 17th-century angler Izaak Walton and prepares a riverside picnic that brings back fond memories and leads to a reflection on the differences between teenage life in their 1970s youth and the present. Setting up home in a narrowbooat, he also prepares a dish Paul's mother used to make - corned beef and potato pie

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian presents a guide to medical dramas, looking back at shows including Emergency Ward 10, Dr Finlay's Casebook, Casualty, Angels and Doctors. Harry assesses each show for the authenticity of sets and costumes, celebrates some of the most frequently recurring tropes of the genre, and reveals what it takes to make a successful hospital drama

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: Documentary about the people living and working in Manchester's remarkable housing boom. A millionaire developer plans to transform the city's red light district into a fashionable residential quarter, as well as taking charge of organising a fundraising concert for a homelessness charity. In Salford, a single mother finds herself priced out of the area she grew up in, and must find a new home for her two young children

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste discuss the personal and social benefits of spreading happiness to others, from video games in church to spending a day as a clown

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate a case of starvation and neglect from 1877 that saw a husband stand trial for the murder of his wife

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle joins a naughty male tiger on a training regime, Kate Humble catches up with a hand-reared cheetah and the staff search for British wildlife living freely on the estate

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich meet Jason and Emily, who want a bohemian fairy garden to match their 18th-century Bristol home

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jonathan Owen from Sussex arrives at the barn, bearing a small item with a big history for the attention of woodwork specialist Will Kirk. The prized possession is a hand-made model Spitfire plane, carved by Jonathan's Uncle Reggie between bombing raids over Germany. The tiny plane has been dearly treasured ever since, but now chipped, with a broken wing and missing part of its propeller, Will has a delicate and important job on his hands. The team also tackles a leather pouffe and a silver purse containing treasured mementos of a beloved mother

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Eastender Edna Siwak and husband Daniel try a week in Melbourne with their baby before deciding whether to move there for good

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke and their guests in a variety of intriguing rounds

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Robshaws get to grips with the home life and food of the 1990s and start the decade delighting in the vast quantities of food now available from around the globe - but soon realise there are definite downsides to the drive towards cheaper and cheaper food of previous years. Giles Coren and food historian Polly Russell introduce them to the culinary treats of the era, from bagged salad and cook-in sauces to organic veg boxes and the gastro pub

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: After learning all about the food of yesteryear, the Robshaws end their time-travelling eating adventure with Giles Coren and Polly Russell introducing them to some potential tastes of the future. Based on the family's experiences of the way culinary history has unravelled, they also make a few predictions about how people will shop, cook and dine over the next 50 years

programme.title: Animals Behaving Badly
programme.desc: In the second episode, Liz Bonnin meets the animals doing whatever it takes to find food, joining experts to uncover the science behind their ingenious tactics and some of their unusual behaviour. Liz sees macaques using psychology to pull off a theft, tigers deceiving their prey, a wedge-billed hummingbird stealing nectar from under the beaks of its rivals, a spider conning prey with a chemical disguise, and coconut crabs who seem intent on a stealing spree

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the Old College in Aberystwyth, Raj Bisram is intrigued by a 19th-century sewing box, and Christina Trevanion is wowed by a sapphire and diamond ring. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin visits a Regency restoration project at the National Botanic Garden of Wales

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of the annual event, which this year takes place at Manchester Regional Arena. The event was originally due to serve as Olympic trials in June, but has twice been postponed due to the coronavirus pandemic. Now it can finally get under way, all eyes will be on the men's and women's 100m, where Ojie Edoburun and Dina Asher-Smith will look to defend their titles. Other disciplines taking place include the men's 110m hurdles and women's 100m hurdles and long jump. With commentary from Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: A visit to Adam Frost's garden, catching up on his new patio project and finding out what his plans are for the autumn. Arit Anderson travels to Somerset to meet landscape designer Dan Pearson, to hear about the design and planting principles he has used to create his own garden. In London, film director and musician Don Letts and his wife reveal how they have combined their different tastes for plants and culture in their town garden. Plus, another chance to see Frances Tophill meet a gardener who grows most of the vegetable ingredients needed for Thai cooking in her back garden and the two enthusiasts with a passion for air plants at their nursery in Lancashire

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian turns his hand to the intricate business of cake art, soon discovering that the devil is in the fiddly detail. A prestigious competition is on the horizon, offering the opportunity for Rhod's ambitious creation to wow the judges - or make people laugh for all the wrong reasons

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Tom and Louise look back on their early love life, and try to decide if they can see a future together. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from the opening contest of the three-match series, which came from Ageas Bowl in Southampton. Australia had not played an international fixture since March, but went into this contest top of the world T20 rankings

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: A report on South Korea's trade in illegal spy camera footage. Pornography has been made illegal in the country, which has led criminals to begin a lucrative trade filming people without their knowledge and distributing the films to strangers or streaming it live on the internet. Stacey investigates the rising number of suicides among victims, examines how the authorities are trying to crack down on this rapidly evolving crime, and questions if the practice could take root in the UK

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Lauren Layfield and Mark Wright build an 'eco den' for 11-year-old Whitney, an avid recycler who takes a bin bag everywhere she goes so she can deal with any litter she finds

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: A strange sea creature that likes to spew out its innards, the mysterious green balls invading an Australian beach and a peculiar pulsating blob found hiding in the city sewers

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: The most unusual places in the UK

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The team reveal the winner of the epic Supercar of the Future competition, look back at what viewers have been up to over summer and have a big surprise in store

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: The crew reaches the remote island of South Georgia, where Steve Backshall witnesses two elephant seals in battle as he walks with the largest colony of king penguins in the world. He's then given the opportunity to swim with the birds as they head offshore to feed, before travelling to his final destination - Antarctica. The naturalist is here with one mission - to dive with a leopard seal, one of the region's greatest predators

programme.title: Rise of the Continents
programme.desc: Iain Stewart examines clues hidden in the landscape to reveal how the Americas were formed from an ancient supercontinent, later named Pangaea. He finds evidence that 300 million years ago New York was at the heart of a huge mountain range, and travels to an extinct volcano in Bolivia to explore subduction - the process that has shaped South America

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The extraordinary abilities of the squirrel family are examined, putting their problem-solving to the test on a specially designed assault course. Some of the world's leading squirrel scientists reveal their groundbreaking discoveries - from the fox squirrel who can remember the location of 9,000 nuts, to the grey squirrel whose tree-top leaps are the basis of new designs in robotics

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As autumn sets in, the farmers prepare for mating season. In Aberdeenshire, deer farmers Isla and George wrangle their herd of semi-wild deer closer to the farm to tackle the challenges of rutting season. In the Lake District, Matt and Murray invest in some new rams, while in Armadale, Joyce Campbell's 600 ewes get stuck in the mud while being herded to the hills. On the Stoer Peninsula, Helen and Graham take their small boat out during settled weather to collect their creel pots

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner joins choreographer Arlene Phillips as she remembers her childhood, and her determination to succeed as a dancer despite a difficult time at school and a great personal loss. He also finds out about the typical food she grew up with, producing two dishes that bring a taste of nostalgia while also paying tribute to her Jewish roots

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's Len Goodman takes naturalist Steve Backshall to the wilds of Dorset to recreate an adventure camping holiday he and his family enjoyed back in 1982

programme.title: Escape to Athena (1979)
programme.desc: Second World War Allied PoWs incarcerated on a Greek island plan to defy the SS by breaking out of prison and pulling off an art robbery, with the help of their liberal-minded camp commandant. Action adventure, with Roger Moore, David Niven, Telly Savalas, Elliott Gould, Stefanie Powers and Claudia Cardinale

programme.title: Dancing Through the Blitz: Blackpool's Big Band Story
programme.desc: Jools Holland, Lucy Worsley and Len Goodman celebrate the big band sound and explore the social history behind the music that saw Britain through the Second World War in a programme originally made to commemorate the 70th anniversary of VE day. They trace the evolution of big band music from its beginnings in 1920s America to its glory days in the three decades that followed. Jools demonstrates how the big band sound developed through the influence of legends such as Duke Ellington, while Len and Lucy explore the dancing that accompanied big bands during the war, like the energetic Jitterbug. Featuring contributions by singer Petula Clark, comedian Roy Hudd, social historian Juliet Gardiner, and John Miller, nephew of bandleader Glenn Miller. Plus, extracts from a performance by Jools Holland and his Rhythm & Blues Orchestra at the Empress Ballroom in Blackpool

programme.title: Animals Behaving Badly
programme.desc: In the final episode, Liz Bonnin meets the animals going to extreme measures to survive, and reveals the some of the surprising scientific discoveries behind their ingenious behaviour. Liz examines the behaviour of cockatoos vandalising houses in Sydney, chimpanzees using bullying tactics to get to the top, crabs who hold anemones hostage to protect themselves, and the stone martens who cause millions of pounds worth of damage to cars while protecting their territories

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by train through Austria and the Czech Republic, beginning in the northern Austrian city of Linz, where Adolf Hitler lived as a young boy. From here, Michael travels onto Prague Central Station, where he meets 87-year-old Zuzana Maresova, who, as a seven-year-old girl climbed aboard a train to travel to safety in London with hundreds of other Jewish children. Eventually crossing the Czech border into Slovakia, Michael reaches his final destination, one of Europe's youngest capital cities, Bratislava

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring initiates a recruitment campaign to attract new blood to Walmington-on-Sea's Home Guard - but makes the mistake of leaving Wilson in charge of the poster. Classic comedy first shown in 1975, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian examines how writers, politicians and artists have manipulated the history of the Spanish Armada. She takes a look at the most famous parts of the story, from Francis Drake's game of bowls to Elizabeth I rousing her troops at Tilbury, and reveals how in Victorian times, the story was used as propaganda for the British Empire

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the high-brow quiz, asking questions about ruffs, red items and other things beginning with the letter R. Trying to come up with the answers are Alan Davies, Tom Allen, Susan Calman and Zoe Lyons

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: First in a two-part pilot for the sci-fi drama. The 12 colonies of man are destroyed by robots, leaving just a few survivors to seek refuge aboard a starship. Guided by President Roslin and Commander Adama, they band together and plan their retaliation, hoping eventually they will stumble across the mythical 13th colony of mankind - Earth. Starring Mary McDonnell and Edward James Olmos

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Concluding part of the pilot for the sci-fi mini-series based on the cult Seventies drama. As the race to outwit the evil Cylons continues, it becomes clear there is a traitor on board - is there any hope of rediscovering the lost planet Earth? Edward James Olmos stars, alongside Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Ginger & Rosa (2012)
programme.desc: Two teenage girls living in 1960s London have been best friends all their lives - ever since their mothers met while giving birth in hospital - and often skip school to spend as much time as they can together. However, their relationship become strained as the Cuban missile crisis places the world on the brink of nuclear war. While one becomes involved in a protest movement, the other seeks meaning in her life through sexual experimentation. Drama, starring Elle Fanning, Alice Englert, Christina Hendricks, Annette Bening and Timothy Spall

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The wildlife film-maker manages to buy the land his badger hide is on, but his excitement is overshadowed by worries about his three-legged pet deer, whose back leg is getting worse. As he tries to accept she may be nearing the end of her life, the villagers ask him to open their local flower show

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: Violin superstars Nicola Benedetti and Alina Ibragimova lead a high-energy evening of baroque music by Vivaldi, Handel, Bach, and little-known English composer Charles Avison. If three double violin concertos weren't enough, there's also a double oboe concerto. With the Orchestra of the Age of Enlightenment, led by Jonathan Cohen. Presented by Danielle de Niese

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Andy Torbet braves one of the most dangerous beaches in Britain, while Nick Hewitt unearths the steel skeletons of two top-secret midget submarines at Aberlady Bay in East Lothian. Nick Crane digs deep to discover what it is like to live on one of the nation's most unusual beaches, the eerily beautiful, vast shingle spit at Dungeness in Kent

programme.title: The Damned United (2009)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama chronicling Brian Clough's ill-fated 44-day tenure as boss of Leeds United in 1974, after Don Revie's departure for the job of England's manager. Following his acrimonious split from Derby County and a brief spell at Brighton & Hove Albion, Clough arrived at Elland Road on a wave of controversy, after previously being an outspoken critic of the club, and struggled to find allies among the playing staff. With Michael Sheen, Timothy Spall, Jim Broadbent and Colm Meaney

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Afua Hirsch explores the cultural history of Kenya, revealing how artists, musicians and writers have responded to its colonial past and asserted a new identity through art

programme.title: Handmade in Africa
programme.desc: Following master beadmaker Jane Semanto in her Nairobi studio as she crafts a traditional Maasai wedding necklace, which is of great ceremonial significance

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The wildlife film-maker manages to buy the land his badger hide is on, but his excitement is overshadowed by worries about his three-legged pet deer, whose back leg is getting worse. As he tries to accept she may be nearing the end of her life, the villagers ask him to open their local flower show

programme.title: The Beauty of Maps
programme.desc: Paul Barber of the British Library discusses the golden age of Dutch cartography and examines the importance of atlases to the commercial and creative endeavours of the 17th century. As maps became a means of expressing power, wealth and control, they grew increasingly elaborate and artistic - and the documentary visits the world's largest atlas, a 24-volume example covering just the Netherlands, and the Klencke Atlas, made for Charles II's restoration in 1660

programme.title: Lost Home Movies of Nazi Germany
programme.desc: Part one of two. Thousands of German attics have become dumping grounds for countless hours of private home movies, left gathering dust for decades and now giving an insight into the innermost recesses of life in Nazi Germany. This programme delves into these private collections, which are only now emerging, to reveal how Germans recorded the regime, featuring films from ordinary citizens as well as Nazi Party organisers, firemen, local officials and soldiers on the frontline

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1977
programme.desc: A selection of outstanding complete archive performances from Britain's Silver Jubilee year. It was a period dominated by funk and punk with Heatwave's Boogie Nights and te Stranglers' No More Heroes in the top 10. Classic top of the charts hits included Baccara with Yes Sir, I Can Boogie and Angelo by Brotherhood of Man. Some of the enduring heroes to take to the stage that year included David Bowie, Rod Stewart, Queen and Elvis Costello, and this showcase also features a rare studio performances from Bob Marley and the Wailers, and the Jacksons

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: At the Royal Albert Hall, Indian sitar player and composer Anoushka Shankar participates in an evening dedicated to her father and musical guru, Ravi, in what would have been his centenary year. Anoushka is joined in the first half by electronic music guru Gold Panda for a new imagining of Ravi's music. In the second half, conductor Jules Buckley, the Britten Sinfonia and soloist Manu Delago accompany Anoushka in a selection of her music. Presented by Josie d'Arby

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: Slavery once drove the economy of the American South and when it was finally abolished in 1863, its gospel tradition lived on. As the black American church grew in strength and popularity, so did its singers. A network of performers emerged known as the gospel highway and in the 1950s, soul music's first generation of stars emerged. The genre delivered an assertive, integrated vision of black America during the Civil Rights era of the early '60s, presided over by the likes of Otis Redding and Aretha Franklin. Narrated by Carleen Anderson

programme.title: Kings of Soul
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the male artists whose vocal stylings have played an instrumental role in the soul genre from the 60s to the 00s. Featuring footage of Brenton Wood performing Gimme Little Sign and Curtis Mayfield singing Keep On Keeping On, as well as appearances by Billy Preston, Bill Withers, Billy Ocean, Alexander O'Neal, Bobby Womack and Barry White

programme.title: Smokey Robinson at the Electric Proms
programme.desc: Trevor Nelson and Edith Bowman introduce a 2009 concert from London's Roundhouse celebrating the 50th anniversary of Motown Records, Smokey Robinson and his band join the BBC Concert Orchestra in new arrangements of old hits and songs from his then-new album Time Flies When You're Having Fun. For 25 years vice president of the company, Robinson was a major factor in its success both as performer with the Miracles and also as songwriter for the Temptations

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe narrates a selection of archive performances to get viewers in the mood for going back to school, featuring the Jackson 5 with ABC, Alice Cooper with School's Out, Busted with What I Go to School For, the cast of Grange Hill with Just Say No and St Winifred's School Choir with No One Quite like Grandma

programme.title: Gregory Porter's Popular Voices
programme.desc: The Grammy award-winning soul and jazz star examines the sound and work of his favourite vocal showstoppers, starting with the world's first pop star, the Italian tenor Enrico Caruso. He moves on to admire the genius of Ella Fitzgerald, the soul of Mahalia Jackson, the power and stagecraft of Freddie Mercury, the artistry and extravagance of Prince, and the modern melisma of Whitney Houston and Mariah Carey. Contributors include Dave Grohl, Beck, Adam Lambert, George Benson, Stephen Costello, Wendy Melvoin and Lisa Coleman

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: Slavery once drove the economy of the American South and when it was finally abolished in 1863, its gospel tradition lived on. As the black American church grew in strength and popularity, so did its singers. A network of performers emerged known as the gospel highway and in the 1950s, soul music's first generation of stars emerged. The genre delivered an assertive, integrated vision of black America during the Civil Rights era of the early '60s, presided over by the likes of Otis Redding and Aretha Franklin. Narrated by Carleen Anderson

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Summer arrives, and in the east of Scotland, Martin prepares two young bulls for the biggest event of the farming calendar - the Royal Highland Show in Edinburgh. First they need their nose rings, an accessory no bull can do without. Martin and Mel's wedding is also looming, and while he goes for his final kilt fitting, Mel meets her Dad to discuss their plans

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin makes a hasty departure from Russia, travelling through Istanbul to Sudan on the third leg of his epic journey around the globe. Along the way he visits a British garrison in Cyprus, works up a sweat in a Turkish bath and mingles with the 3,000 guests at a Greek Orthodox wedding

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Saga and Martin continue to hunt down the eco-terrorists, who realise they are being followed by the police and proceed with caution. Meanwhile, Jakob realises that living with his girlfriend is not always easy, and Martin makes progress with Jens. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Saga and Martin set out to track down the leader of the eco-terrorists, hoping to stop any further attacks being planned. Meanwhile, Martin hits it off with his Danish colleague Pernille. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The investigation grows, offering up new leads. Meanwhile, four people wearing animal masks carry out a new attack, before posting footage of the assault online. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Saga visits Martin and his ailing son at the hospital and notices something is not quite right about the place. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1977
programme.desc: A selection of outstanding complete archive performances from Britain's Silver Jubilee year. It was a period dominated by funk and punk with Heatwave's Boogie Nights and te Stranglers' No More Heroes in the top 10. Classic top of the charts hits included Baccara with Yes Sir, I Can Boogie and Angelo by Brotherhood of Man. Some of the enduring heroes to take to the stage that year included David Bowie, Rod Stewart, Queen and Elvis Costello, and this showcase also features a rare studio performances from Bob Marley and the Wailers, and the Jacksons

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Summer arrives, and in the east of Scotland, Martin prepares two young bulls for the biggest event of the farming calendar - the Royal Highland Show in Edinburgh. programme.title: Lost Home Movies of Nazi Germany
programme.desc: Part two of two. Amateur footage from the latter half of the Second World War, offering an insight into life and atrocities in Germany and the occupied territories as Hitler's dreams crumble and the moral reckoning the German people had to face. The films reveal the stories of people battling to save their families from deportation to the death camps, while others endure the horrors of ever more deadly bombing raids, all set against a backdrop of propaganda and false hope pouring forth from Nazi high command
