programme.desc: It's custard Pie day in Biggleton, but there's a problem with the custard delivery

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble is feeling sad because he has lost Tumble Ted. Justin and his friend are feeling very happy - playing music, dressing up and dancing with their friends

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: Nick and Norman are at the seaside and someone has left a plastic bag on the beach

programme.title: Oti's Boogie Beebies
programme.desc: In this episode, Oti teaches us to walk through the jungle and look for animals!

programme.title: CBeebies Little Red Riding Hood
programme.desc: Join Maddie, Nigel, the Woodland Keepers and Northern Ballet for Little Red Riding Hood

programme.title: YolanDa's Band Jam
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown welcomes berimbau player Oren Marshall and his capoeira players

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Cat prepares to set off on another big adventure, but must first unpack the contents of her Cat-pack, which has become so full it is too heavy to carry

programme.title: Tee and Mo Song Time
programme.desc: Tee and Mo get ready to leave the house and turn the action into a song and dance number, as they remember things they will need on their day out

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day with a flying school for animals. In Antarctica a wandering albatross chick masters the largest wings of any bird. Swinging through the rainforest, lar gibbons seem to be flying. Great grey owl chicks leap from the top of huge trees

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's the hottest day of the year and Waffle is eating ice cream to cool down. Outside in the sweltering heat, Simon turns on the sprinkler to help everyone cool down, but poor Waffle doesn't like the sprinkler and darts through the hole in the fence to Mrs Hobbs's garden, looking for shade. The Brooklyn-Bells try to coax him back, but he refuses to return. Doug decides to make a mini swimming pool by filling the ball pit with cool water. Waffle loves swimming pools. Maybe that will encourage him back?

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Tim takes grandma's dog Mossy out for a walk, but worries that he might have set off too late for her to reach her favourite bush in time to go to the toilet

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Eddie, whose mum is Italian, makes an authentic Italian cheese and olive pizza for his friends, using home-made dough and cooking it outside in his family's pizza oven

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: The children learn about their families, their heritage and their family trees. Bella is worried her family might be boring compared with Sam and Mali's clans

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: Mum and Melody fly a kite, and later the youngster asks for some music about hills or mountains

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: The Patchwork Pals have a problem: today is their lazy day, but Patchwork Fly won't let them rest! What is he up to?

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm visits a farm with over 8,000 chickens, and JB goes to a hill farm to help with their cows. Plus, children in Leeds builds ponds, and youngsters in Scotland pick raspberries

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: Stories about the everyday lives of children living in eight different countries around the world. In this programme we visit three children who are all having fun. In Ghana, Tyra, who's five, is helping her mum cook tea along with little sister Tracy. In Canada, eight-year-old Rhys is learning how to ice skate - and taking a few tumbles along the way. And in Italy, seven-year-old Mario is escaping from the hot sun by playing a diving game in the swimming pool with his brother and sister

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today we meet a new family. Brother and sister, Noah and Isla, are playing at being train drivers with their cuddly toys as the passengers. The pretend train is off to London where the toys get to meet Queen Isla

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo and his friends encourage young viewers to question the world around them, beginning by exploring things that are big - elephants, trees, cities and planets

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Rory goes to meet Esme and Bingham the bearded dragon. They clean out the vivarium and provide fresh food, water and calcium to keep Bingham's bones strong

programme.title: Monty & Co
programme.desc: Lulu wants to make a castle for her princess dolls. At first she rejects help from Eddie, but she later sees the sense in working together as a team

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble helps Grandad Tumble to water his vegetables, while Justin and his friends have fun gardening and making a scarecrow

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back 220 million years to the time of the coelophysis - one of the earliest dinosaurs that hunted in packs and was an expert at fishing

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns how a lamp works and visits a factory to see how a playhouse is made.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter is forced to reveal the location of the secret treehouse to Flopsy and Mopsy - just as Old Brown launches an attack

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: An electrical storm strikes Academy Island, and the Go Jetters are trapped!

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo has a sleepover at Gran Gran's, but bedtime comes while it's still light outside.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's the hottest day of the year and Waffle is eating ice cream to cool down. Outside in the sweltering heat, Simon turns on the sprinkler to help everyone cool down, but poor Waffle doesn't like the sprinkler and darts through the hole in the fence to Mrs Hobbs's garden, looking for shade. The Brooklyn-Bells try to coax him back, but he refuses to return. Doug decides to make a mini swimming pool by filling the ball pit with cool water. Waffle loves swimming pools. Maybe that will encourage him back?

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with eggbox and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: At Home with Mr Tumble
programme.desc: Join Mr Tumble at home, where today he has been playing musical statues!

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Barry demonstrates just how daring he really is by exploring the wonders beneath a magical trapdoor, but Kari is unsure about his tales of a land filled with glitter and unicorns

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: How Lion found his roar by entering the Great Cave of Tinga Tinga

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy heads to the Amazon rainforest to take a picture of a coati for Jen

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Liam and Katie Morag choose special presents for Grannie Island on Grandmother's Day, but their plan to deliver them to her are threatened

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The librarian loses his big reference book and Sarah thinks her toy periscope might help with the search

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Mini Reader arrives at school wearing a beautiful pair of glasses, causing Lola to reconsider her attempts to avoid a trip to the optician's

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee is super-excited when new flat-pack shelving arrives for his room. However, he soon discovers it's going take some time for Mo to build it. Pre-school animation about a baby monkey and his mother.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny and Small discover the froglets sitting abnormally silent and calm in their cave, in front of three stones which glow blue and emit an unearthly sound. Major Clanger think these must be 'hush stones' however the effect is anything but calming

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos ride on the Pinky Ponk, but it bumps into a tree and the gazebo. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: George Ezra reads Under the Same Sky by Britta Teckentrup, a story about the hopes and dreams that we all experience under the same sky.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle joins a naughty male tiger on a training regime, Kate Humble catches up with a hand-reared cheetah and the staff search for British wildlife living freely on the estate

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts revisits a Derbyshire development project overseen by a husband and wife that he first came across in 2016. Plus, the progress of properties in Bootle and Preston

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A Welshman who relocated to Shetland is admitted to A&E with a very sore shoulder

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, as Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help the teams look for items to sell at auction

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A heartrending death devastates some of the Towers residents. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Lifestyle presenters Piers Taylor, Tom Dyckhoff, Esme Young, Danny Clarke and Keith Brymer Jones take on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the south Wales countryside to help a couple with £500,000 to spend on relocating from Newport to a village community. While in the region, Nicki visits the only whisky distillery in Wales that uses the mineral-rich water of the Brecon Beacons - to potent effect

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich meet Jason and Emily, who want a bohemian fairy garden to match their 18th-century Bristol home

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton travel across Derbyshire and Leicestershire, where items of interest include 18th-century carvings and a bowl full of marbles. Along the way Catherine gets steamed up on the heritage Great Central Railway and James visits a pottery

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Jessie Wallace and Lacey Turner, better known to fans of the show as Kat and Stacey, for a Slater family reunion. They talk about their characters' most memorable moments, reveal some of their best behind-the-scenes stories and give an idea of what's to come when the soap returns next week

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back at when the celebrities have been pushed to the limits, from trying to impress some of the best chefs in the country to the most daunting challenge of all, the Chefs' Table. They also recall a couple of memorable challenges, including a final week task from 2012 in which Christine Hamilton, Dick Strawbridge and Lisa Faulkner had to prepare a fine dining lunch from a tiny kitchen on the Orient Express

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man is found murdered on his boat five miles out at sea, with no possible way a killer could have escaped. At first Humphrey turns his attention to the small group he was with the night before he died - but as the investigation deepens, the investigation takes an unexpected turn that requires the team to leave the comfort of their Caribbean island - and go searching for their suspects in London. Kris Marshall stars

programme.title: Match of the Day Wales
programme.desc: Finland v Wales. Jason Mohammad presents highlights of both teams' opening fixture in Group B4 of the UEFA Nations League. With analysis from Kit Symons and Jess Fishlock

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a selection of eight of the most extraordinary sequences from Planet Earth II and Blue Planet II, with a new soundtrack by Hans Zimmer, Jacob Shea and the team at Bleeding Fingers - performed by the BBC Concert Orchestra and Mercury Award-winning rapper Dave on the grand piano. The film ranges from the East Cape of South Africa to the north of Norway and from Chile to the Indian Ocean to show how animals overcome adversity to survive and thrive in some of the world's most challenging environments

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason and Adam Fleming present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Most people who go missing are reported to police within a few hours, but for those who live on the streets it isn't always so straightforward. Aaron hasn't been seen for two months when the homeless healthcare centre that usually treats him notifies Gloucestershire Constabulary that he is a missing person. Officers scour the streets and homeless shelters but can find no trace of him. The town's rough sleepers know him well, but no one has any idea what may have happened to him. It's not long before another drug user is added to the missing priority list - James, who is of no fixed address, has discharged himself from hospital against medical advice. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Jon Kay presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: It is the tiger cubs' first birthday bash, and Ben Fogle and Kate Humble look for signs that Longleat Safari Park's Southern Koala breeding programme - the only one in Europe - has resulted in a joey. Meanwhile, Jean Johansson joins keeper James to take a closer look at the anatomy of Gwen - a huge hairy tarantula

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is in Croydon, south London, at a basement flat bought by two brothers. Martel Maxwell visits a colourful terrace in Cumbria, while Dion Dublin is in Greater Manchester, where a first-time developer has taken on a real challenge

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the UK's most remote hospital. There are serious concerns for a baby boy who is brought into A&E feeling very unwell

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles arrives in Peterborough for one of the largest antiques fairs in the UK. He's joined by Bargain Hunt regular Colin Young, who has some top tips for porcelain collectors. Experts Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell guide the red and blue teams around the fair, with each side hoping to buy low and sell high

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: With the community grieving, some residents consider leaving, and Watto has the last word at the wake. Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke star

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Soap actors Lisa Maxwell, Ayden Callaghan, Sean Wilson, Scott Wright and Steven Arnold compete in a charity edition hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple who have £500,000 to make a much-yearned-for leap to a country life on the Welsh borders. Along the way, the presenter visits 17th-century Tredegar House, now home to local artists' studios, and lends a hand making traditional Welsh musical instruments

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich try to breathe life into a concrete courtyard in Greater Manchester. Nick and Dinah want cottage plants, vegetable beds and room for family meals

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: James Braxton and Catherine Southon hit the final leg of their challenge, travelling through Cambridgeshire and Suffolk before heading to auction at Lincolnshire. Which one of them will make the greater profit?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and adventurer explores some Wales' most intriguing and hard-to-access caves. From a smugglers den to an ancient bone cave and from a revolutionary 19th-century hide-out to one of the finest decorated caves in Europe, Will reveals the subterranean wonders of Wales few realise even exist

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley presents a compilation of the best unseen moments from her time interviewing Walford's biggest stars

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker asks the questions in the light-hearted quiz, with celebrity guests AP McCoy, Ugo Monye, Jade Jones and Gary Pallister joining team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: Bolton's funniest stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian becomes a policeman with South Wales Police, spending time with traffic officers and taking part in a drugs raid. However, he soon appreciates that there is a big difference between life on stage and life on the beat

programme.title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy (2011)
programme.desc: George Smiley, the deputy to the head of British intelligence, is forced into retirement after an operation ends in disaster with the death of an MI6 agent. Smiley is later recruited by the government to return to duty and investigate the possibility of a Soviet mole in a high-ranking position within MI6. Cold War thriller based on John le Carré's 1974 novel, starring an Oscar-nominated Gary Oldman, alongside Colin Firth, Tom Hardy, John Hurt, Benedict Cumberbatch, Toby Jones, Mark Strong, Ciaran Hinds and Roger Lloyd Pack

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by podcast-presenting couple Chris and Rosie Ramsey and chef Yotam Ottolenghi. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson treats her supper guests to a feast of lamb chops with mint, chilli and potatoes, served alongside roasted red onions with fennel and basil. Dessert is a true Anglo-Italian affair with a plum and amaretti crumble. There are also recipes for farro risotto with mushrooms, and chocolate pasta with a dark butterscotch sauce

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is by the seaside in Norfolk, Martel Maxwell encounters a house with no stairs in Lancashire and Dion Dublin is in Staffordshire at a home with a carpeted bath

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of day two of the annual event, which this year takes place at Manchester Regional Arena. Holly Bradshaw is aiming to win her eighth national pole vault title and local favourite Naomi Ogbeta will hope to make an impact in the triple jump, while the men's and women's 200m finals take place on the track. With commentary from Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Alice Through the Looking Glass (2016)
programme.desc: Alice Kingsleigh has spent three years sailing the high seas. Upon her return to London from China, she returns to the magical world of Underland and takes a journey back in time to save the Mad Hatter and her friends. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Johnny Depp and Anne Hathaway

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a psychiatrist from London, a drama student from Leicester, and a bus driver from Derbyshire

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a couples special of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants including Harry Judd, Izzy Judd, Ade Adepitan, Elle Exxe, Dan Renton Skinner, Olivia Lee, Nik Speakman and Eva Speakman

programme.title: Invictus (2009)
programme.desc: Following his election as president of South Africa, Nelson Mandela finds himself the leader of a racially and economically divided nation, and seeks a means to unite the people. The country's entry in the 1995 Rugby World Cup provides a perfect opportunity, and he enlists the aid of captain Francois Pienaar in making his team an inspiration to all South Africans. Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Morgan Freeman and Matt Damon

programme.title: Don't Take Me Home
programme.desc: A look at how Wales fared at Euro 2016, the first time they had qualified for a major international football tournament since the 1958 World Cup. Featuring match highlights and interviews with the players and coaching staff.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Nikki Fox, Miles Jupp, Nish Kumar and Joe Lycett to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is disappointed to discover she is not invited to Maria's hen party because of her age, and decides to gatecrash the event, only to end up at the wrong venue. She then tries to impress the bride-to-be's mother during a dinner date, but is so nervous she turns to drink, and the evening descends into chaos. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich assist Emma and Steve, whose front garden in Nottinghamshire overlooking the River Trent, feels very much 'on display' to passers-by. The couple want greater privacy without losing the precious river view and also wish to utilise a half-boat that they own in the design. Both Charlie and the siblings propose a similar clever idea to solve the privacy problem, a sunken courtyard garden. This only makes it harder for Emma and Steve to decide which design to pick

programme.desc: Bolton's funniest stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is disappointed to discover she is not invited to Maria's hen party because of her age, and decides to gatecrash the event, only to end up at the wrong venue. She then tries to impress the bride-to-be's mother during a dinner date, but is so nervous she turns to drink, and the evening descends into chaos. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy (2011)
programme.desc: George Smiley, the deputy to the head of British intelligence, is forced into retirement after an operation ends in disaster with the death of an MI6 agent. Smiley is later recruited by the government to return to duty and investigate the possibility of a Soviet mole in a high-ranking position within MI6. Cold War thriller based on John le Carré's 1974 novel, starring an Oscar-nominated Gary Oldman, alongside Colin Firth, Tom Hardy, John Hurt, Benedict Cumberbatch, Toby Jones, Mark Strong, Ciaran Hinds and Roger Lloyd Pack

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by podcast-presenting couple Chris and Rosie Ramsey and chef Yotam Ottolenghi. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson treats her supper guests to a feast of lamb chops with mint, chilli and potatoes, served alongside roasted red onions with fennel and basil. Dessert is a true Anglo-Italian affair with a plum and amaretti crumble. There are also recipes for farro risotto with mushrooms, and chocolate pasta with a dark butterscotch sauce

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is by the seaside in Norfolk, Martel Maxwell encounters a house with no stairs in Lancashire and Dion Dublin is in Staffordshire at a home with a carpeted bath

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of day two of the annual event, which this year takes place at Manchester Regional Arena. Holly Bradshaw is aiming to win her eighth national pole vault title and local favourite Naomi Ogbeta will hope to make an impact in the triple jump, while the men's and women's 200m finals take place on the track. With commentary from Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Alice Through the Looking Glass (2016)
programme.desc: Alice Kingsleigh has spent three years sailing the high seas. Upon her return to London from China, she returns to the magical world of Underland and takes a journey back in time to save the Mad Hatter and her friends. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Johnny Depp and Anne Hathaway

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a psychiatrist from London, a drama student from Leicester, and a bus driver from Derbyshire

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a couples special of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants including Harry Judd, Izzy Judd, Ade Adepitan, Elle Exxe, Dan Renton Skinner, Olivia Lee, Nik Speakman and Eva Speakman

programme.title: Invictus (2009)
programme.desc: Following his election as president of South Africa, Nelson Mandela finds himself the leader of a racially and economically divided nation, and seeks a means to unite the people. The country's entry in the 1995 Rugby World Cup provides a perfect opportunity, and he enlists the aid of captain Francois Pienaar in making his team an inspiration to all South Africans. Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Morgan Freeman and Matt Damon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Nikki Fox, Miles Jupp, Nish Kumar and Joe Lycett to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Z for Zachariah (2015)
programme.desc: A woman lives alone on a farm that belonged to her family in the aftermath of a nuclear apocalypse, believing herself to be the last survivor of the human race. Her simple life is disrupted by the appearance of a second survivor, and they adapt to working together - but the arrival of a third individual threatens everything they have built. Sci-fi drama based on Robert C O'Brien's novel, starring Margot Robbie, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Chris Pine

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle joins a naughty male tiger on a training regime, Kate Humble catches up with a hand-reared cheetah and the staff search for British wildlife living freely on the estate

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts revisits a Derbyshire development project overseen by a husband and wife that he first came across in 2016. Plus, the progress of properties in Bootle and Preston

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A Welshman who relocated to Shetland is admitted to A&E with a very sore shoulder

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, as Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help the teams look for items to sell at auction

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A heartrending death devastates some of the Towers residents. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Lifestyle presenters Piers Taylor, Tom Dyckhoff, Esme Young, Danny Clarke and Keith Brymer Jones take on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the south Wales countryside to help a couple with £500,000 to spend on relocating from Newport to a village community. While in the region, Nicki visits the only whisky distillery in Wales that uses the mineral-rich water of the Brecon Beacons - to potent effect

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich meet Jason and Emily, who want a bohemian fairy garden to match their 18th-century Bristol home

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton travel across Derbyshire and Leicestershire, where items of interest include 18th-century carvings and a bowl full of marbles. Along the way Catherine gets steamed up on the heritage Great Central Railway and James visits a pottery

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Jessie Wallace and Lacey Turner, better known to fans of the show as Kat and Stacey, for a Slater family reunion. They talk about their characters' most memorable moments, reveal some of their best behind-the-scenes stories and give an idea of what's to come when the soap returns next week

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back at when the celebrities have been pushed to the limits, from trying to impress some of the best chefs in the country to the most daunting challenge of all, the Chefs' Table. They also recall a couple of memorable challenges, including a final week task from 2012 in which Christine Hamilton, Dick Strawbridge and Lisa Faulkner had to prepare a fine dining lunch from a tiny kitchen on the Orient Express

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man is found murdered on his boat five miles out at sea, with no possible way a killer could have escaped. At first Humphrey turns his attention to the small group he was with the night before he died - but as the investigation deepens, the investigation takes an unexpected turn that requires the team to leave the comfort of their Caribbean island - and go searching for their suspects in London. Kris Marshall stars

programme.title: Searching for Shergar
programme.desc: The story of one of the world's most valuable racehorses, Shergar, which was famously abducted from its stables in 1983, at the height of the Troubles, and never seen again. Thirty seven years on, Alison Millar sets out to see if changed times will help her unearth the mystery once and for all

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a selection of eight of the most extraordinary sequences from Planet Earth II and Blue Planet II, with a new soundtrack by Hans Zimmer, Jacob Shea and the team at Bleeding Fingers - performed by the BBC Concert Orchestra and Mercury Award-winning rapper Dave on the grand piano. The film ranges from the East Cape of South Africa to the north of Norway and from Chile to the Indian Ocean to show how animals overcome adversity to survive and thrive in some of the world's most challenging environments

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason and Adam Fleming present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Jon Kay presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: It is the tiger cubs' first birthday bash, and Ben Fogle and Kate Humble look for signs that Longleat Safari Park's Southern Koala breeding programme - the only one in Europe - has resulted in a joey. Meanwhile, Jean Johansson joins keeper James to take a closer look at the anatomy of Gwen - a huge hairy tarantula

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is in Croydon, south London, at a basement flat bought by two brothers. Martel Maxwell visits a colourful terrace in Cumbria, while Dion Dublin is in Greater Manchester, where a first-time developer has taken on a real challenge

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the UK's most remote hospital. There are serious concerns for a baby boy who is brought into A&E feeling very unwell

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles arrives in Peterborough for one of the largest antiques fairs in the UK. He's joined by Bargain Hunt regular Colin Young, who has some top tips for porcelain collectors. Experts Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell guide the red and blue teams around the fair, with each side hoping to buy low and sell high

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: With the community grieving, some residents consider leaving, and Watto has the last word at the wake. Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke star

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Soap actors Lisa Maxwell, Ayden Callaghan, Sean Wilson, Scott Wright and Steven Arnold compete in a charity edition hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple who have £500,000 to make a much-yearned-for leap to a country life on the Welsh borders. Along the way, the presenter visits 17th-century Tredegar House, now home to local artists' studios, and lends a hand making traditional Welsh musical instruments

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich try to breathe life into a concrete courtyard in Greater Manchester. Nick and Dinah want cottage plants, vegetable beds and room for family meals

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: James Braxton and Catherine Southon hit the final leg of their challenge, travelling through Cambridgeshire and Suffolk before heading to auction at Lincolnshire. Which one of them will make the greater profit?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich assist Emma and Steve, whose front garden in Nottinghamshire overlooking the River Trent, feels very much 'on display' to passers-by. The couple want greater privacy without losing the precious river view and also wish to utilise a half-boat that they own in the design. Both Charlie and the siblings propose a similar clever idea to solve the privacy problem, a sunken courtyard garden. This only makes it harder for Emma and Steve to decide which design to pick

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley presents a compilation of the best unseen moments from her time interviewing Walford's biggest stars

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker asks the questions in the light-hearted quiz, with celebrity guests AP McCoy, Ugo Monye, Jade Jones and Gary Pallister joining team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: Keepin 'er Country
programme.desc: The fortunes of some of Ireland's leading line dancers at the biggest competitions at home and abroad, from the rigour of rehearsal to the glamour of the finals

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: End-of-summer edition of the comedy panel show, with regulars Colin Murphy, Jake O'Kane, Neil Delamere and Tim McGarry giving their views on the 'new normal', as they poke fun at how society is coping with the pandemic. The show has a slightly different, with some lucky viewers joining in from the comfort of their own homes. From the White House to the big house on the hill - will anyone escape the panellists' caustic wit?

programme.title: Match of the Day from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Romania v Northern Ireland. Stephen Watson presents action from both teams' opening fixture in Group B1 of the competition. With analysis from Jim Magilton

programme.title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy (2011)
programme.desc: George Smiley, the deputy to the head of British intelligence, is forced into retirement after an operation ends in disaster with the death of an MI6 agent. programme.title: Judge Rinder

New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Simon, Duncan, Michelle and Annabel pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale Family Tree
programme.desc: A look back at the farming family who have been right at the heart of the soap since it launched in 1972. With Annie Sugden at the helm this family have endured heartbreak and in-fighting like no other. From feuding brothers Joe and Jack to fierce sibling rivalry between Robert and Andy, it has never been easy being a Sugden

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: The Government has promised to ban the sale of all new diesel, petrol and hybrid cars in the UK by 2035. But will people - and the necessary technology - be ready for the electric revolution? Motoring journalist Ginny Buckley investigates

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: For the final leg of his journey along the Wales Coast Path, Sean Fletcher explores the sand dunes at Merthyr Mawr on horseback. He also discovers how sea cliffs breathe in and out with the pounding of waves and re-creates Marconi's first radio transmission over open water, from Lavernock Point to Flatholm island

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: This episode focuses on products that have not changed in centuries, including Heck sausages, which are produced on a family farm in Bedale, North Yorkshire. There is also a look at the Maldon salt company, who have been producing sea salt since the late 19th century, and Glenfarclas single malt whisky, which has been made by the Grant family in the Scottish Highlands since 1865

programme.title: Black Work
programme.desc: A suspect is taken into custody for Ryan's murder, but Jo is not convinced the police have the guilty party. She continues to investigate her husband's undercover work, discovering dark secrets relating to both his colleagues and the criminals they were pursuing, and finally coming to a disturbing revelation about the man she married

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary camera footage, including a climber in Colorado who took a 40ft fall - with every terrifying moment captured on his bodycam - and a Northern Ireland family whose holiday to Greece was ruined by a freak storm. Also, the story of a paralysed man who set a world record by walking a marathon, a Transport for London employee who came up with a knockout idea to liven up his station announcements and a Bristol couple who tried to free a trapped turtle in Oman

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A worn-down Amy reveals to Marlon that Al's threatening to sack her because of a missing delivery of pizza boxes, while Jamie overhears Sam bad-mouthing him. The social worker enters in the midst of a row between Charity and Sarah, and Belle arrives back at the Dingle's to collect her things after confessing to Zak that she's moving in with Jamie

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff plays the victim once again, but Tim wonders if his dad is as innocent as he makes out, while Mary and Cathy discuss Dev's money problems. Daniel admits to Adam that he's been paying Nicky to dress up as Sinead so he could pretend she's still alive, and David assures Shona that she's improving all the time

programme.title: James Nesbitt: A Game of Two Halves
programme.desc: The accomplished actor embarks on an epic journey to see how Soccer Aid for Unicef is helping children around the world. Since 2006, Soccer Aid has raised over £38million and James will see just how far that money goes. His mission is to take essential supplies to children living in three extreme locations - the Himalayas in Nepal, the desert surrounding the Dead Sea and a remote island in the South Pacific. For James however, the journey is one into the unknown, as he won't know where he is going on each leg of his mission until just before he leaves

programme.title: Black Work
programme.desc: Jo is left reeling as the secrets she has uncovered prove devastating for her family. She decides to abandon her investigation into Ryan's death out of fear she is endangering her children. However, when another body is discovered, she realises that her husband's murderer is still at large, and it is up to her to see that he is brought to justice. But as she takes up the case again, she does not realise that the killer could be terrifyingly close to home. Conclusion of the three-part crime drama, starring Sheridan Smith

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Back in April, as the country went into lockdown, Ant and Dec presented this year's Britain's Got Talent auditions, knowing that the finals would have to be postponed until later in the year. Now the series finally returns, as judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams decide which acts will make it through to the semi-finals

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Nick Cannon hosts the final of the celebrity singing contest, with the three remaining mystery stars to be crowned this year's winner. As ever, regular judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger will be on hand to assess the performances and try to guess who is hiding behind the elaborate costumes

programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by guests Marvin Humes and Suzi Perry, and chefs Daniel Clifford and Bettina Campolucci Bordi. James serves up meatballs, soufflé, quiche and chicken chipotle, as well as providing a Masterclass in honeycomb

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by Alison Hammond and Aldo Zilli in the first episode of a new cookery series, which focuses on dishes that conjure up memories of holidays. Ainsley prepares spicy Seafood linguine with chilli and cherry tomatoes, Alison makes a Tunisian inspired lamb and cous cous dish, and Aldo gives a masterclass in making crispy chilli and parsley calamari. Finally, Ainsley also whips up a souvlaki and tiramisu

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits the region of Jura, where a local farmer shows him around his orchards and teaches him about the region's apples and pears, which James uses as the accompaniment in a barbecued duck. He also visits Fort des Rousses, the old military stronghold that is now used for the making of Comte, one of the country's most popular cheeses

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of this afternoon's meetings, including the 2.35 September Stakes from Kempton Park, the 4.05 Sprint Cup from Haydock Park and action from Ascot. programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Perry Fenwick, Jenny Eclair, Ore Oduba and Richard Madeley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the first semi-final of this year's talent contest, making a return to screens following the auditions episodes broadcast in the spring. Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo are on the judging desk, as the acts that impressed them the most return, including the golden buzzer acts - singer Fayth Ifil, comedian and singer Jon Courtney, choir Sign Along With Us, comedian Nabil Abdulrashid, and mother and daughter singing duo, Honey and Sammy. Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Vinnie Jones joins Piers Morgan for an intimate Life Stories special, 10 years on from his first appearance on the show. Vinnie opens up about how his life has changed irrevocably in that time, as he comes to terms with grieving for the loss of his wife, Tanya, who passed away last year, after a six-year battle with cancer. He also looks back at some of the highlights of his football and movie careers, talks about his struggles with alcohol, how he is hoping to help other people, particularly men, to cope with grief and what the future now holds

programme.title: UEFA Nations League Highlights
programme.desc: Iceland v England. Mark Pougatch presents action from both teams' opening Group A2 encounter at Laugardalsvollur in Reykjavík. Plus, the key moments from Denmark v Belgium and Sweden v France. With analysis from Ian Wright and Roy Keane and reports by Gabriel Clarke, Richard Henwood and Celina Hinchcliffe, and commentary from Sam Matterface, Lee Dixon, Joe Speight and Seb Hutchinson

programme.title: James Nesbitt: A Game of Two Halves
programme.desc: The accomplished actor embarks on an epic journey to see how Soccer Aid for Unicef is helping children around the world. Since 2006, Soccer Aid has raised over £38million and James will see just how far that money goes. His mission is to take essential supplies to children living in three extreme locations - the Himalayas in Nepal, the desert surrounding the Dead Sea and a remote island in the South Pacific. For James however, the journey is one into the unknown, as he won't know where he is going on each leg of his mission until just before he leaves

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage eight of the most prestigious race on the calendar, which featured a mountainous 141km route from Cazeres-sur-Garonne to Loudenvielle. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Small Fortune
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Dermot O'Leary, in which teams of friends from London and ballroom dancers from Glasgow take on challenges in miniature settings to win a cash prize of up to £150,000

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Emmerdale Family Tree
programme.desc: A look back at the farming family who have been right at the heart of the soap since it launched in 1972. With Annie Sugden at the helm this family have endured heartbreak and in-fighting like no other. From feuding brothers Joe and Jack to fierce sibling rivalry between Robert and Andy, it has never been easy being a Sugden

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: The Government has promised to ban the sale of all new diesel, petrol and hybrid cars in the UK by 2035. But will people - and the necessary technology - be ready for the electric revolution? Motoring journalist Ginny Buckley investigates

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: For the final leg of his journey along the Wales Coast Path, Sean Fletcher explores the sand dunes at Merthyr Mawr on horseback. He also discovers how sea cliffs breathe in and out with the pounding of waves and re-creates Marconi's first radio transmission over open water, from Lavernock Point to Flatholm island

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: This episode focuses on products that have not changed in centuries, including Heck sausages, which are produced on a family farm in Bedale, North Yorkshire. There is also a look at the Maldon salt company, who have been producing sea salt since the late 19th century, and Glenfarclas single malt whisky, which has been made by the Grant family in the Scottish Highlands since 1865

programme.title: Black Work
programme.desc: A suspect is taken into custody for Ryan's murder, but Jo is not convinced the police have the guilty party. She continues to investigate her husband's undercover work, discovering dark secrets relating to both his colleagues and the criminals they were pursuing, and finally coming to a disturbing revelation about the man she married

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary camera footage, including a climber in Colorado who took a 40ft fall - with every terrifying moment captured on his bodycam - and a Northern Ireland family whose holiday to Greece was ruined by a freak storm. Also, the story of a paralysed man who set a world record by walking a marathon, a Transport for London employee who came up with a knockout idea to liven up his station announcements and a Bristol couple who tried to free a trapped turtle in Oman

programme.desc: A worn-down Amy reveals to Marlon that Al's threatening to sack her because of a missing delivery of pizza boxes, while Jamie overhears Sam bad-mouthing him. programme.desc: Geoff plays the victim once again, but Tim wonders if his dad is as innocent as he makes out, while Mary and Cathy discuss Dev's money problems. programme.title: James Nesbitt: A Game of Two Halves

programme.title: Black Work
programme.desc: Jo is left reeling as the secrets she has uncovered prove devastating for her family. She decides to abandon her investigation into Ryan's death out of fear she is endangering her children. However, when another body is discovered, she realises that her husband's murderer is still at large, and it is up to her to see that he is brought to justice. But as she takes up the case again, she does not realise that the killer could be terrifyingly close to home. Conclusion of the three-part crime drama, starring Sheridan Smith

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Back in April, as the country went into lockdown, Ant and Dec presented this year's Britain's Got Talent auditions, knowing that the finals would have to be postponed until later in the year. programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by Alison Hammond and Aldo Zilli in the first episode of a new cookery series, which focuses on dishes that conjure up memories of holidays. programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure

programme.desc: Sally considers a job which will take her away from the family home. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.desc: George seeks solace when his budgie dies, but Mildred is too preoccupied with finding something good enough to donate to the jumble sale. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.desc: Tony and Kevin celebrate when the bank accepts their offer on the garage. Gail receives an alarming call from the police. Emily is nearly knocked over by Samantha on her motorbike

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role

programme.desc: A new recruit reports for his first day of work at the police station, but given his family's history in Aidensfield, not all of the villagers are pleased to see him. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A police lab technician is stabbed to death in her home and while Brooks and Casey pursue their lead suspect, another woman is attacked in an identical fashion. The second victim survives and is able to identify her assailant, but she and Casey fall for each other - a relationship that threatens to damage both the CPS's case and the detective's career. Guest starring Lydia Leonard, with Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Cleaning Up
programme.desc: Terrified that her identity will be revealed, Sam navigates her way through the Network meeting. Promising Swanny that she can fill Blake's shoes, she is left with the impossible task of acquiring secret information about an upcoming property deal. Sam ropes in Jess and Mina to help with her biggest scam yet, but things do not go according to plan. Elsewhere, Sam deals with pressures at home, as she tries to prove to her daughters how much she truly cares for them. Starring Sheridan Smith and Jade Anouka

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A man accused of murdering five people is killed in prison shortly before going to trial, wrapping up a lot of loose ends and saving the state the expense of keeping him behind bars indefinitely. Alas, Morse cannot help wondering why the body of one of his victims was never found - and the chance discovery of a corpse gives him the perfect opportunity to investigate further. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star

programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman

programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Hypochondriac Malcolm Rayner is struggling with his breathing and Martin asks him if he has ever worked with asbestos. Penhale then causes pandemonium by putting up warning notices about the substance's harmful properties throughout the village. Meanwhile, Bert pops the question to Jennifer, and Ruth finds fault with Al's business proposal. Guest starring Christopher Fairbank (Auf Wiedersehen, Pet)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor becomes convinced something is wrong with him following a conversation with his mother, and Louisa's attempts at getting him to open up about the loss of his father prove futile. Mrs Tishell goes to the surgery for advice regarding a pain in her jaw, only to find herself falling in love with Martin all over again. Elsewhere, Al receives a visit from two military policemen who are trying to track down Mike Pruddy

programme.title: Cleaning Up
programme.desc: Swanny entrusts Sam to make a large investment for him, and she goes to desperate lengths to pull off the extraordinarily risky task. As Sam revels in her new persona, Dave starts to notice the change in her, and Glynn finds out that Sam is still in financial trouble. Meanwhile, Jess and Mina are both put under pressure to come clean as the investigation into illegal activity in the office comes to a head, but it is Sam who takes the wrap. Starring Sheridan Smith, Jade Anouka and Matthew McNulty

programme.title: Cleaning Up
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Sam's world falls apart when her landlady returns with some devastating news, and she is forced to face the disastrous consequences of her actions. Coming clean to Dave could solve Sam's problems, but she risks losing her family. Meanwhile, Jess is left heartbroken when her business plan falls through. With no job and no home, Sam moves in with Jess and together they decide to concoct a brilliant but dangerous idea to try and get their lives back on track

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a retired university fellow leads to a complex investigation for Morse and Lewis as they piece together a story of student suicide, drugs and the mysterious dismissal of a loyal college scout. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Phyllis Logan, Gabrielle Lloyd, Amanda Ryan and Tony Haygarth

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: Bless This House

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Johnny's past returns to haunt him when Scott tells Jenny all about the crime they committed more than 40 years ago, while temperatures rise at Speed Daal when Geoff turns up for work. Having returned from their meeting, Abi thanks Peter for his support, but Debbie is watching from a distance, with her suspicions heightened

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya is furious to find an alcohol delivery on the doorstep of Speed Daal and refuses point blank to let Geoff buy her out of the business, leading him to resort to sabotage. David arranges to take Shona for a walk, but it doesn't go quite to plan, while Daniel apologises to Ray for causing a scene in the bistro

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff plays the victim once again, but Tim wonders if his dad is as innocent as he makes out, while Mary and Cathy discuss Dev's money problems. Daniel admits to Adam that he's been paying Nicky to dress up as Sinead so he could pretend she's still alive, and David assures Shona that she's improving all the time

programme.title: The Great St Trinian's Train Robbery (1966)
programme.desc: A crook and his cronies stash the money from a train heist in a deserted old mansion, planning to recover it in good time. However, several years later, when the thieves arrive to collect their loot, they discover the building has been turned into a school for girls where the pupils have become infamous for their escapades. Comedy, starring Frankie Howerd, Dora Bryan, Reg Varney and George Cole

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oxford's last-surviving all-female college is holding a reunion to bid farewell to one of its most prominent professors. However, the evening leads to tragedy when a guest is found murdered, and Lewis is convinced the killing is connected to an attack that occurred at the same institution 10 years previously. The detective calls in his former DS, Alison McLennan, to help with the investigation, but the case takes a twist when two more bodies turn up. Crime drama, guest starring Juliet Stevenson and Saskia Reeves, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The crime drama returns, with Barnaby and Winter joined on the year's first case by a new pathologist, Petra Antonescu (Anamaria Marinca). A woman slips away from a Jane Austen re-enactment in full costume, only to be stabbed to death in the woods - her murder being recorded by a camera drone. The victim is discovered to be an undercover journalist, who had some unusual questions to ask about the village and the country house where the historical event took place. Guest starring Claire Skinner and Samuel West

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a student from a prestigious all-female college is found dead, Will and Geordie infiltrate the murky world of campus politics and university societies to find the killer. Navigating strange initiations, raucous parties - and with investigative journalist, Ellie Harding, hot on their heels - they need to use every advantage at their disposal to unravel the mystery. Cathy's ambitions for a life of her own start to test the delicate family balance, while fresh from a spell with Daniel in Marrakech, Leonard finds it hard to acclimatise to life back in Grantchester. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: After Will and Leonard witness a deadly and deliberate hit-and-run, Geordie traces the car to a pair of decidedly dysfunctional brothers, whose house lies far off the beaten path. The victim's wife, his employee and the two brothers are all damaged characters caught in a web of lies and betrayals - and it's not just secrets hiding within the siblings' grounds. Back at home, Will and Geordie both struggle as unwelcome guests look set to stay - Will's mum Amelia seems to have found a carbon copy of his late father in her new beau. Meanwhile, a new television set lands in the vicarage - just in time for Eurovision. Robson Green, Tom Brittney and Al Weaver star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The murder of a caretaker has been so carefully planned and executed that the police are left without a single piece of evidence. Adding to the mystery is the fact that the crime is laid out in detail in a manual purchased three weeks earlier in the victim's name. Brooks and Casey eventually track down the killer, who confesses, but the trial is put in jeopardy by a canny defence lawyer. Guest starring Toby Stephens

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A murder inquiry is launched when the police are alerted to shocking internet footage showing a teenage girl being attacked and shot. The investigation is hindered by the lack of a body, but the video provides enough clues to eventually put a name to the victim and track down her killers. However, a vow of silence between the defendants means Jake and Alesha face one of their toughest court battles yet

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The team tries to juggle three cases over the course of one day, beginning at 6am when Brooks and Casey are called to a crime scene where a barman has been shot dead in his car. Then, a family dispute leads to murder, and the frenetic morning takes an unexpected turn when an allegation of police intimidation is made against one of the officers. Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike takes up photography, and persuades Sid to let him use the old garage for his new hobby. Family comedy, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Robin Stewart

programme.desc: Ida tries to cause trouble for Sally. Becky starts to enjoy being with Des and Claire. Jim is upset to discover Andy is planning to move out and live with Anne

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emma goes back to school for the first time since Geri's birth. Tony is in a rush to complete the purchase of Woodside Farm. Steve advises Frank to sell some of his assets

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve apologises to Zoe. Biff prepares to see his dad for the first time. Sophie and Zoe's relationship grows stronger, but there is an unexpected shock in store for them

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl disappears, prompting the police to begin a desperate search, convinced her errant father has snatched her. Blaketon decides to lend his expertise to the case - only to end up fighting for his life. Meanwhile, Crane and Bellamy's dedication to duty sees them placing their lives on the line to rescue the youngster - but tragedy strikes. James Carlton, Mark Jordon and Derek Fowlds star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally considers a job which will take her away from the family home. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid believes he is in for a fortune when a friend mentions him in his will. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George seeks solace when his budgie dies, but Mildred is too preoccupied with finding something good enough to donate to the jumble sale. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new recruit reports for his first day of work at the police station, but given his family's history in Aidensfield, not all of the villagers are pleased to see him. Meanwhile, Ben and Liz's fund-raising efforts appear to have rubbed off on Vernon, who begins to show his charitable side

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank explains to Chris and Zoe why he has been released from prison. Viv is fed up of looking after the business. Zoe arranges a date with Sophie

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda does not realise why Biff is upset. The Dingles are fed up of Albert's cooking. Chris witnesses Zoe say a fond farewell to Sophie when she leaves for Zurich

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sean warns Des that Claire probably just wants a father for Becky. Kevin informs Tony he wants to buy the garage. Jim tells Steve he is going to set up a business for them

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tony and Kevin celebrate when the bank accepts their offer on the garage. Gail receives an alarming call from the police. Emily is nearly knocked over by Samantha on her motorbike

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new recruit reports for his first day of work at the police station, but given his family's history in Aidensfield, not all of the villagers are pleased to see him. Meanwhile, Ben and Liz's fund-raising efforts appear to have rubbed off on Vernon, who begins to show his charitable side

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A police lab technician is stabbed to death in her home and while Brooks and Casey pursue their lead suspect, another woman is attacked in an identical fashion. The second victim survives and is able to identify her assailant, but she and Casey fall for each other - a relationship that threatens to damage both the CPS's case and the detective's career. Guest starring Lydia Leonard, with Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Cleaning Up
programme.desc: Terrified that her identity will be revealed, Sam navigates her way through the Network meeting. Promising Swanny that she can fill Blake's shoes, she is left with the impossible task of acquiring secret information about an upcoming property deal. Sam ropes in Jess and Mina to help with her biggest scam yet, but things do not go according to plan. Elsewhere, Sam deals with pressures at home, as she tries to prove to her daughters how much she truly cares for them. Starring Sheridan Smith and Jade Anouka

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A man accused of murdering five people is killed in prison shortly before going to trial, wrapping up a lot of loose ends and saving the state the expense of keeping him behind bars indefinitely. Alas, Morse cannot help wondering why the body of one of his victims was never found - and the chance discovery of a corpse gives him the perfect opportunity to investigate further. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman
